no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes requirements (define SPI, integrate with LocalNode, wire ServiceLoader, update BUILD files) without specifying exact code changes, line numbers, or literal patches. It describes what to build, not how to fix a specific bug.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert gold-specific implementation details: exact method name 'executeWithInterceptors' (test_execute_with_interceptors_method_exists), exact reverse loop pattern with specific variable names (test_interceptor_chaining_builds_from_last_to_first), exact field declaration pattern 'private final List<NodeCommandInterceptor> interceptors', exact variable name 'interceptor' in 'interceptor.isEnabled(config)'. A correct alternative using different helper method names, different iteration patter

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Almost every test is a regex search over source files (e.g., re.search(pattern, content)). The only behavioral tests are TestBazelCompilation which runs bazel build, but the fail_to_pass tests are all pure text-matching: test_interceptor_has_isEnabled_method, test_builder_has_add_interceptor_method, test_interceptor_chaining_builds_from_last_to_first, etc. No test imports/calls the Java code or verifies runtime behavior of the interceptor chain.

test_not_tautological.reason: Since tests only check for text patterns in source files (regex matches on file contents), a stub implementation that contains the right method signatures but empty bodies would pass all the non-compilation tests. For example, an executeWithInterceptors that just returns null with the right signature and the right loop pattern would pass test_interceptor_chaining_builds_from_last_to_first. The Bazel compilation tests provide some guard, but the core behavioral requirements (interceptors actually

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction says 'A single interceptor receives every WebDriver command executed through the node' and 'Multiple interceptors are invoked in the correct order' — these runtime behaviors are never actually tested. Only text patterns and compilation are checked, not actual command interception behavior.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized into descriptive classes (TestNodeCommandInterceptorInterface, TestLocalNodeInterceptorSupport, TestLocalNodeFactoryLoadsInterceptors, etc.) with descriptive docstrings and method names.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: No obvious typos in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in the instruction or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction explicitly names 'LocalNode', 'Builder', 'addInterceptor', 'META-INF/services/', 'ServiceLoader.load(...)', 'isEnabled(config)', 'initialize(config, eventBus)', and specifies exact method signatures and integration points. While some specificity is needed, naming the exact factory class (LocalNodeFactory) and method names goes beyond describing the problem.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-implementation task, not a config-edit task.
